headline: Clemson survives Oklahoma shootout in turnover-marred thriller
body: Clemson escaped with a 28-27 victory over Oklahoma in a game that turned entirely on ball security. Both offenses moved the field with ease, combining for 784 yards and six touchdown passes, but Oklahoma's three fumbles proved lethal in a one-point decision. Glass found Walsh for 119 yards and a score, and the Oklahoma passing game was crisp enough to keep pace with Clemson's efficient attack, yet those turnovers created a gap neither Glass nor the offense could quite close.

James Cortez orchestrated the winning performance with composure and precision, completing 27 of 37 passes for 388 yards and three touchdowns. His poise in the pocket was the difference maker—he never turned the ball over and avoided the pressure mistakes that plagued Glass. Clemson's defense, anchored by Carter's sack and six tackles, applied just enough resistance to force Oklahoma into mistakes when it mattered most. The Clemson secondary bent but didn't break, holding its ground as Glass aired it out 47 times.

What could have been a showcase offensive clinic became a cautionary tale about turnover margin. Oklahoma dominated the line of scrimmage statistically, gaining 413 total yards to Clemson's 397, and actually out-rushed Clemson 42 to 21. But yardage doesn't account for those three fumbles that shifted momentum at critical junctures. Clemson will leave Norman knowing they won a game they didn't necessarily play better, a margin of luck that won't sustain them long term but proved decisive on this October afternoon.
turningPoint: Oklahoma's third fumble in the fourth quarter, deep in Clemson territory with the game hanging in the balance, squandered what could have been a go-ahead score and handed Clemson the final possession it needed.

unsungHero.name: Fields
unsungHero.why: Fields caught six passes for 107 yards and two touchdowns—equaling half of Clemson's passing production—yet Cortez earned Player of the Game because his turnover-free execution defined the victory.
